English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
John H. Nixon papers
Nixon, John H.
6336
4 cubic feet. 4 cubic feet.
1952-1995. Research Materials, Printed Materials Reports and studies deriving from Nixon's work as a consulting economist. Collection material in English 3332274
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Meaghan Corbett
Date completed:
July 2007
EAD encoding:
Meaghan Corbett, July 2007
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Economist.
Ralph M. Parsons, Company, Washington and Los Angeles.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Reports and studies deriving from Nixon's work as a consulting economist with particular reference to the Hong Kong Airport, Cairo water studies, and other projects. Also includes early files on New York State economy and politics, 1950s; "Research Memoranda" from the Committee for Economic Development, Area Development Division by Nixon and others, 1960s; works on real estate indexation, 1990s; and speeches and articles.
Cite As:
John H. Nixon papers, #6336. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
New York (State) -- Economic policy. Real property. Planning -- Egypt -- Cairo. Planning -- China -- Hong Kong. Water-supply. Airport -- Design and construction.
